rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory development for Study 4, where the authors argue that brief exposure to indulgent food cues can whet appetite and increase reward-seeking. This is not just a background list citation; it helps justify their duration-based prediction (H3) that short scent exposure should increase unhealthy choices. They then empirically test that prediction, linking their observed short-exposure effect to this prior sampling-based reward-seeking logic.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Wadhwa et al.'s cue-induced reward-seeking account by testing it in an ambient-scent context and introducing exposure duration as a boundary condition. It shows that brief indulgent scent exposure produces the predicted increase in unhealthy choice, while longer exposure reverses the effect.
